chunk_summary: This section discusses pediatric growth classification and nutritional triage using WHO and IAP growth charts, emphasizing macronutrient decisions based on growth patterns and clinical indicators for children under 5 and those aged 5–18 years.
learning_objectives: Understand the use of WHO growth standards for children under 5 and IAP growth charts for children aged 5–18 years.
Identify key growth indicators such as stunting, wasting, and overweight using z-scores and BMI-for-age.
Learn to triage pediatric nutritional needs based on growth patterns and clinical red flags.
Apply appropriate interventions for stunting, wasting, and other growth deviations in children.
key_facts: WHO growth standards use z-scores: stunting is height-for-age < -2 SD, wasting is weight-for-height < -2 SD, and overweight is weight-for-height > +2 SD.
IAP 2015 growth charts for 5–18 years use BMI-for-age with adult-equivalent cut-offs: 23 for overweight risk and 27 for obesity risk in South Asian children.
Stunting with normal weight-for-height requires diet diversity and infection control, not short-term calorie increases unless wasting is present.
Negative energy balance (e.g., weight crossing down percentiles) requires intake quantification, red flag screening, and outpatient or inpatient care decisions.
Mini-case example: A 10-month-old with weight-for-length -3 SD and diarrhea needs dehydration care and energy-dense feeds during recovery.
reference_quote: Revised IAP Growth Charts for Height, Weight and Body Mass Index for 5- to 18-year-old Indian Children (Indian Pediatrics, 2015).
document_key_topics: WHO and IAP growth standards for children
